title: xDots Showcases Room-Temperature Quantum Sensing System at Quantum Korea 2026 - Quantum Computing Report
gist: Startup xDots demoed xEnergy at Quantum Korea 2026, a diamond NV-center room-temperature quantum sensing stack that maps current flow at ±0.01% precision and, paired with multi-agent AI optimization, delivered verified 15-30% energy reductions in Korean industrial pilots with SPC, Kolon, and Hoban.

insights.claim: xDots' xSee sensor uses diamond nitrogen-vacancy (NV) center technology to map micro-fluctuations in magnetic fields and electrical currents at ±0.01% precision, operating natively at room temperature and eliminating the dilution refrigerators and liquid helium cooling required by alternative quantum systems.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Room-temperature operation removes the biggest deployment cost of quantum hardware, enabling industrial field use; a key commercialization advantage.
insights.quote: the diamond NV paradigm functions natively at room temperature, entirely eliminating the bulky, high-maintenance dilution refrigerators and liquid helium cooling hardware
insights.locator: The hardware foundation of the framework

insights.claim: The xSee sub-system captures live telemetry at ±0.01% precision, measuring microscopic voltage spikes and residual current drops that remain undetected by classical transformers.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Detecting losses invisible to classical instruments is the value proposition for energy-efficiency buyers; verify precision claims independently.
insights.locator: The sub-system captures live telemetry loops

insights.claim: xEnergy integrates quantum sensing (xSee) with a multi-agent AI stack (xMon visibility dashboard and xOpt autonomous optimizer) to address power hyper-inflation in massive production plants and high-density data centers.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Targeting data-center power inflation ties quantum sensing to the AI-compute energy-demand boom; a topical adjacency for investors.
insights.locator: At the center of the exhibition, xDots demonstrated

insights.claim: xDots' proof-of-concept pilots with South Korean conglomerates confirmed absolute energy reductions of 15-30%, including predictive refrigeration maintenance with SPC Secta9ine, pump diagnostics with Kolon Global, and active HVAC balancing with Hoban Construction's Island Resom.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Named enterprise pilots with quantified 15-30% savings are concrete commercial traction; watch for conversion to paid scaled deployments.
insights.quote: These projects confirmed absolute energy reductions of 15–30%
insights.locator: To validate the platform’s utility before scaling

insights.claim: Quantum Korea 2026, hosted by South Korea's Ministry of Science and ICT at Seoul's Dongdaemun Design Plaza, gathered 56 corporations and research institutions across 12 countries under the theme 'Quantum in Action, Grand Challenges for Innovation.'
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Signals Korea's growing role as a quantum commercialization hub; a venue to track for emerging vendors and government priorities.
